title: Compositions and methods for improving plant health and controlling plant disease
abstract: Compositions and methods for controlling a plant pest or for treating or preventing plant disease are provided. Such compositions and methods comprise a bacterial strain that controls one or more plant pests or that improves at least one agronomic trait of interest in a plant. The bacterial strain can be used as an inoculant for plants. Methods for controlling a plant pest, for growing a plant susceptible to a plant disease, and for controlling plant disease on a plant susceptible to the plant disease are provided. Methods for improving at least one agronomic trait of interest in a plant are also provided.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A harvested plant or plant part comprising a biocontrol agent, wherein the biocontrol agent comprises a bacterial strain deposited as NRRL No. B-50897 or NRRL No. B-50999. 2. The harvested plant or plant part of claim 1, wherein the biocontrol agent is present at about 10 5 CFU/gram to about 10 12 CFU/gram, at about 10 7 total cells/gram to about 10 14 total cells/gram, at about 10 5 CFU/ml to about 10 12 CFU/ml, or at about 10 7 total cells/ml to about 10 14 total cells/ml. 3. The harvested plant or plant part of claim 1 or 2, further comprising a pesticide, a fungicide, a bactericide, a nematicide, an insecticide or an herbicide. 4. The harvested plant or plant part of claim 3, wherein the fungicide comprises prothioconazole, azoxystrobin, fluopicolide, chlorothalonil, fosetyl, fenhexamid, flutriafol, difenoconazole, tebuconazole, tetraconazole, pyraclostrobin, trifloxystrobin, propiconazole, fluoxastrobin, flutolanil, metconazole, a copper compound, or metrafenone. 5. The harvested plant or plant part of any one of claims 1-4, further comprising a second biocontrol agent. 6. The harvested plant or plant part of claim 5, wherein the second biocontrol agent comprises Bacillus amyloliquefaciens strain D747, Bacillus subtilis strain QST 713, Bacillus amyloliquefaciens strain GB03, Bacillus subtilis strain FB17, Bacillus pumilus strain QST 2808, Bacillus pumilus strain GB34, Bacillus subtilis var. amyloliquefaciens strain FZB24, Bacillus finnus strain CNMC 1-1582, Streptomyces lydicus strain WYEC108, Streptomyces griseoviridis strain K61, Agrobacterium radiobacter strain 1026, Agrobacterium radiobacter strain K84, Pseudomonas fluorescens strain A506, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies aizawai strain GC-91, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain EG7841, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain SA-12, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain ABTS-351, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain SA-11, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies tenebrionis strain SA-10, Chromobacterium subtsugae strain PRAA4-1, Isaria fumosorosea Apopka Strain 97, Burkholderia spp. strain A396, or Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies aizawai strain ABTS-1857. 7. The harvested plant or plant part of any one of claims 1-6, wherein said harvested plant part comprises an apple, strawberry, peach, sweet cherry, or Bing cherry fruit. 8. The harvested plant or plant part of any one of claims 1-7, wherein said harvested plant or plant part has reduced decay from a plant pathogen as compared to a harvested plant or plant part not comprising said biocontrol agent. 9. The harvested plant or plant part of claim 8, wherein said plant pathogen is selected from the group consisting of Erwinia spp., Pseudomonas spp., Xanthomonas spp, Moniliana spp., Botrytis spp., Penicillium spp., and Rhizopus spp. 10. The harvested plant or plant part of claim 8, wherein said plant pathogen is selected from the group consisting of Acidovorax avenae, Burkholderia gladioli, Candidatus Liberibacter spp., Erwinia amylovora, Erwinia ananas, Erwinia chrysanthemi, Erwinia dissolvens, Erwinia herbicola, Erwinia rhapontic, Erwinia stewartiii, Erwinia tracheiphila, Erwinia uredovora; Pectobacterium carotovorum, Pseudomonas syringae, Streptomyces scabies, Xanthomonas campestris, Xanthomonas axonopodis, Xanthomonas fragariae; Xanthomonas translucens, Erwinia amylovora, Moniliana fructicola, Botrytis cinerea, Rhizopus stolonifera, and Xylella fastidiosa. 11. A method of controlling a plant pest comprising applying to a plant or a plant part after harvest a biocontrol agent, wherein the biocontrol agent comprises: (a) an effective amount of a bacterial strain deposited as NRRL No. B-50897 or NRRL No. B- 50999; or (b) an effective amount of a supernatant, fermentation product, filtrate, or extract derived from a whole cell culture of a bacterial strain deposited as NRRL No. B-50897 or NRRL No. B-50999; wherein said effective amount controls the plant pest. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the plant pest is an insect, nematode, fungus, fungal -like organism, virus, viroid, bacterium, or a protozoan pathogen. 13. The method of claim 11 or 12, wherein said plant pest is Erwinia spp., Pseudomonas spp., Xanthomonas spp, Moniliana spp., Botrytis spp., Penicillium spp., and Rhizopus spp. 14. The method of claim 11 or 12, wherein said plant pest is selected from the group consisting of Acidovorax avenae, Burkholderia gladioli, Candidatus Liberibacter spp., Erwinia amylovora, Erwinia ananas, Erwinia chrysanthemi, Erwinia dissolvens, Erwinia herbicola, Erwinia rhapontic, Erwinia stewartiii, Erwinia tracheiphila, Erwinia uredovora; Pectobacterium carotovorum, Pseudomonas syringae, Streptomyces scabies, Xanthomonas campestris, Xanthomonas axonopodis, Xanthomonas fragariae; Xanthomonas translucens, Erwinia amylovora, Moniliana fructicola, Botrytis cinerea, Rhizopus stolonifera, and Xylella fastidiosa. 15. The method of any one of claims 11-14, wherein the biocontrol agent is applied by dipping, drenching, flooding, fogging, spraying, dusting, or injecting. 16. The method of any one of claims 11-14, wherein said effective amount of the bacterial strain comprises at least about 10 6 to 10 12 CFU/100 gallons of water or at least about 10 8 to 10 14 total cells/ 100 gallons of water. 17. The method of any one of claims 11-16, wherein a second biocontrol agent is applied to said plant or plant part. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the second biocontrol agent comprises Bacillus amyloliquefaciens strain D747, Bacillus subtilis strain QST 713, Bacillus amyloliquefaciens strain GB03, Bacillus subtilis strain FB17, Bacillus pumilus strain QST 2808, Bacillus pumilus strain GB34, Bacillus subtilis var. amyloliquefaciens strain FZB24, Bacillus finnus strain CNMC 1-1582, Streptomyces lydicus strain WYEC108, Streptomyces griseoviridis strain K61, Agrobacterium radiobacter strain 1026, Agrobacterium radiobacter strain K84, Pseudomonas fluorescens strain A506, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies aizawai strain GC-91, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain EG7841, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain SA-12, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain ABTS-351, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain SA-11, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies tenebrionis strain SA-10, Chromobacterium subtsugae strain PRAA4-1, Isaria fumosorosea Apopka Strain 97, Burkholderia spp. strain A396, or Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies aizawai strain ABTS-1857. 19. The method of claim 17 or 18, wherein the biocontrol agent and the second biocontrol agent are applied simultaneously. 20 The method of claim 17 or 18, wherein the biocontrol agent and the second biocontrol agent are applied sequentially. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein said second biocontrol agent is applied pre-harvest to said plant or plant part. 22. The method of claim 20, wherein said second biocontrol agent is applied post-harvest to said plant or plant part. 23. The method of any one of claims 11-22, wherein said plant part comprises an apple, strawberry, peach, sweet cherry, or Bing cherry fruit. 24. A method of treating or preventing a plant disease comprising applying to a plant or plant part a biocontrol agent after harvest, wherein the biocontrol agent comprises: (a) an effective amount of a biocontrol agent comprising a bacterial strain deposited as NRRL No. B- 50897 or NRRL No. B-50999; or (b) an effective amount of a supernatant, fermentation product, filtrate, or extract derived from a whole cell culture of a bacterial strain deposited as NRRL No. B-50897 or NRRL No. B-50999; wherein the effective amount treats or prevents the plant disease. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein said effective amount of the bacterial strain comprises at least about 10 4 to 10 16 CFU per hectare or at least about 10 6 to 10 18 total cells per hectare. 26. The method of claim 24 or 25, wherein the plant disease is Asian Soybean Rust (ASR), gray mold, leaf spot, Frogeye Leaf Spot, Early Blight, Damping off complex, Brown Patch, black scurf, root rot, belly rot, Sheath Blight, Powdery Mildew, Anthracnose, Black Sigatoka, Anthracnose leaf spot, Downy Mildew, Pythium Blight, Late Blight, Fusarium Head Blight, sudden death syndrome (SDS), Fusarium Wilt, Com Stalk Rot, Brown Rust, Black Rust, Yellow Rust, Wheat Rust, Rust, Apple Scab, Post-bloom Fruit Drop, Gummy Stem Blight, Greasy Spot, Com Stalk Rot, Cherry Blossom Blight, Damping Off, Fire Blight, Citrus Greening Disease, Clubroot, Verticillium Wilt, Rhizopus Rot, Blue Mold, or Brown Rot. 27. The method of any one of claims 24-26, wherein the biocontrol agent is applied by dipping, drenching, flooding, fogging, spraying, dusting, or injecting. 28. The method of any one of claims 24-27, wherein said effective amount of the bacterial strain comprises at least about 10 6 to 10 12 CFU/100 gallons of water or at least about 10 8 to 10 14 total cells/ 100 gallons of water. 29. The method of any one of claims 24-28, wherein a second biocontrol agent is applied to said plant or plant part. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the second biocontrol agent comprises Bacillus amyloliquefaciens strain D747, Bacillus subtilis strain QST 713, Bacillus amyloliquefaciens strain GB03, Bacillus subtilis strain FB17, Bacillus pumilus strain QST 2808, Bacillus pumilus strain GB34, Bacillus subtilis var. amyloliquefaciens strain FZB24, Bacillus finnus strain CNMC 1-1582, Streptomyces lydicus strain WYEC108, Streptomyces griseoviridis strain K61, Agrobacterium radiobacter strain 1026, Agrobacterium radiobacter strain K84, Pseudomonas fluorescens strain A506, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies aizawai strain GC-91, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain EG7841, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain SA-12, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain ABTS-351, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies kurstaki strain SA-11, Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies tenebrionis strain SA-10, Chromobacterium subtsugae strain PRAA4-1, Isaria fumosorosea Apopka Strain 97, Burkholderia spp. strain A396, or Bacillus thuringiensis subspecies aizawai strain ABTS-1857. 31. The method of claim 29 or 30, wherein the biocontrol agent and the second biocontrol agent are applied simultaneously. 32. The method of claim 29 or 30, wherein the biocontrol agent and the second biocontrol agent are applied sequentially. 33. The method of any one of claims 24-32, wherein said plant part comprises an apple, strawberry, peach, sweet cherry, or Bing cherry fruit.
